Tests for the behavior of the Math.min function with various numeric inputs, including NaN and Infinity.

# Purpose
This code is a test suite for the `Math.min` function in JavaScript. It uses assertions to verify that `Math.min` correctly identifies the smallest value among its arguments, including handling special cases such as `NaN`, `Infinity`, and `-Infinity`. The tests ensure that when `NaN` is an argument, the result is `NaN`, and when comparing positive and negative zero, the result is `-0.0`. The code also checks that `Math.min` returns the smallest finite number when compared with `Infinity` and `-Infinity`. This script provides narrow functionality focused on validating the behavior of the `Math.min` function under various conditions.
